exemplar.title: Will did Oreo by Mondelez International adapt their strategies to sustain profitability during the global health crisis?

detailedExemplarData.title: Will did Oreo by Mondelez International adapt their strategies to sustain profitability during the global health crisis?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly identifies and defines the key concept of creativity
Makes regular links between campaign decisions and the concept
Demonstrates good understanding with clear analytical points
weaknesses: Discussion sometimes becomes descriptive rather than analytic
Lacks an academic model or citation to deepen the concept definition
Integration of creativity is not sustained throughout every section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows good understanding and periodically analyses Oreoâs activities through the lens of creativity. However, the discussion often reverts to description without fully weaving the concept into every analytical point. This performance aligns best with band 3 (good understanding and clear analytical thinking) rather than bands 4â5.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents are listed, all directly related to Oreo/Mondelez
External data (e.g. revenue comparisons) inform key tables and claims
weaknesses: Citation formatting is inconsistent and unclear (merged footnotes, OCR artifacts)
Depth of data extraction is limited, with little critical interrogation of figures
Numbering and labels in the bibliography and ToC are inconsistent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student provides the correct number of relevant documents, the uneven citation style and shallow depth of extraction place this work at band 2 (documents generally relevant but lacking depth) rather than band 3.

strengths: Selection of core tools (7Ps, positioning map, profitability ratios) is appropriate
Marketing-mix and ratio tools are introduced with definitions
weaknesses: Some financial figures appear implausible or inconsistent, undermining credibility
Methodological steps (e.g. profitâmargin formulas) are not always shown
Relevance of certain tool applications to the research question is only superficial
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student selects suitable theories and tools but applies them with errors and without transparent methodology. The performance aligns with band 2 (some tools selected, superficial relevance) rather than higher bands.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Incorporates a range of financial data and positions Oreo on a product lifecycle map
Recognizes Oreoâs maturity stage and links it to marketing activity
weaknesses: Analysis frequently summarizes data without deeper interrogation or benchmarks
Several claims (e.g. TikTok impact, gross profit > sales) lack supporting numbers or context
Positioning map axes are unlabeled, and currency conversions are not explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis shows some understanding of the data but is often descriptive and contains errors. This corresponds to band 2 (limited analysis of the research question) rather than band 3 or above.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question in the conclusion
Refers back to the three main analytical tools
weaknesses: Draws on unverified or inconsistent data in making final claims
Introduces new factors (e.g. Ukraine war) that were not analysed earlier
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are partially consistent with the presented evidence and explicitly answer the question, fitting band 2 (some conclusions consistent with evidence, partial answer) rather than full consistency.

strengths: Follows a clear IB structure with introduction, analysis, conclusion, bibliography, appendices
Wellâorganized table of contents and coherent section headings
weaknesses: Appendix headings use inconsistent styles and sizes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report maintains an appropriate and logical structure throughout, meeting band 2 requirements (appropriate structure) with only minor formatting inconsistencies.

strengths: Includes all required IA elements on the title page (RQ, word count, concept)
Figures and tables are correctly labeled and captioned
weaknesses: Footnote and citation formatting is inconsistent with OCR artifacts
Some superscripts and table captions are incorrectly placed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation elements are present and functional, but formatting errors and inconsistencies place this at band 2 (all required elements included with minor issues).

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical IB-style structure (introduction, analysis, conclusion, bibliography, appendices). Headings and table of contents enhance coherence, fulfilling the descriptor for âappropriate structure throughout.â

Most documents are Mondelez financial reports or reputable business articles and directly inform tables or claims; depth is adequate but not extensive, with limited critical extraction of figures, so relevance is solid rather than outstanding.
All required IA elementsâtitle page with RQ, word count, section headings, references and appendicesâare present, satisfying the highest presentation strand band.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed and labelled, satisfying the 3â5 document requirement. Citation format is uneven but documents are clearly distinguishable.

Application of each tool is generally soundâe.g., ratios calculated and interpreted, marketing-mix linked to social-media campaignâbut some figures appear implausible (sales revenue lower than profit), suggesting only adequate rather than fully effective application.

The student presents primary financial data tables and a positioning map; while some numerical accuracy is doubtful, a reasonable range of data is incorporated and referenced, meeting âadequateâ selection and use.

Analysis frequently summarises rather than interrogates data (e.g., stating ratios are âexcellentâ without benchmarking detail) and repeats descriptive points about campaigns. There is limited depth and integration, aligning with âlimited analysis of the research question.â
The research question is answered explicitly in the final paragraph with a clear âyesâ, supported, albeit imperfectly, by preceding discussion.

The student selects three core, syllabus-relevant tools (7Ps focus, positioning map, profitability ratios). These cover both marketing and finance and are appropriate to the RQ, demonstrating effective breadth.
Conclusions broadly align with presented evidence but rest on some unverified numbers and sweeping statements; they demonstrate partial, not full, consistency with the analysis.

All analytical tools are consistently applied to Mondelez/Oreo; marketing-mix, positioning map and ratio analysis are tied back to sales data and campaign performance. This shows strong, organisation-specific analysis, though on occasion claims (e.g., exact revenue differences with Lotus) are not fully evidenced, preventing a âcomprehensive integrationâ judgement.

The student clearly identifies âcreativityâ as the key concept, defines it and periodically links campaign decisions (e.g., the âStay Playful at Homeâ promotion) to this concept. While references to creativity appear in each main section, the discussion often becomes descriptive rather than fully woven into the analytical narrative. Integration is present but not sustained throughout, matching the descriptor for a good, but not strongly or fully integrated, understanding.

annotations.comment: The research question is restated here but the sentence structure is awkward. Consider: âThis study will analyse whether Oreoâs strategiesâ¦â.
The marketing mix tool is introduced with a relevant definition, demonstrating sound selection of theory for analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The price range data for Oreo in Malaysia is descriptive but the source citation (footnote 7) is unclear. Provide a more precise reference or table.
The discussion of the âStay Playful At Homeâ promotion is descriptive; link back to creativity by explaining how original ideas drove engagement.
The claim that 2047 TikTok posts âincreased their salesâ is asserted without data. Introduce specific sales figures or benchmarks to support this analysis.
Identifying Oreoâs maturity stage during the pandemic is insightful, showing adequate data selection to support product lifecycle analysis.
The net revenue figures ($7.66 billion vs $28.7 billion) lack context and appear inconsistent. Clarify whether these are segment vs total figures and reconcile them.
Consider embedding the actual positioning map graphic here rather than only describing it later in the appendices, to strengthen tool application.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The superscript for 2021 is placed inside a math tag incorrectly. Use a consistent citation format and separate equations from footnotes.
The positioning map figure is correctly labeled and captioned, meeting presentation expectations for visual aids.
The axes of the positioning map lack unit labels and scales. Include clear axes titles and scales to improve interpretability.
The title page clearly includes all required IA elementsâtitle, research question, key concept, session and word countâdemonstrating strong adherence to presentation requirements.
The research question contains a grammatical error (âWill did Oreoâ¦â). Revise for clarity, e.g., âDid Oreo by Mondelez International adaptâ¦â to improve precision.
The concept of creativity is named but would benefit from a more academic definition and citation. Consider referencing a theory or model to deepen integration.
The table of contents is well organized with clear headings, enhancing navigability and demonstrating appropriate structure.
Supporting documents are listed in the ToC but the numbering and labels are inconsistent. Standardize titles and numbering for clarity.
The introduction provides context on Oreo and the pandemic, effectively setting up the studyâs focus and linking to the research question.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent and includes OCR artifacts (e.g., âISUPPORTING DOCUMENT 11â). Clean up citations for better presentation.
The definition of creativity is present but not yet linked analytically to specific campaign decisions. Expand analysis by showing how creativity underpinned strategy.
The explanation of profit margins is clear and relevant, demonstrating effective selection of financial analysis tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Connect the creativity concept to how Oreoâs quality and price positioning was differentiated from competitors on the map.
The comparison with Lotus Biscoff uses revenue figures that seem inconsistent. Provide source details and verify currency conversions.
The revenue comparison draws on supporting documents effectively, showing appropriate use of external data.
Currency mix of USD and Euros is presented without exchange rates, making direct comparisons unreliable. Include conversion methodology.
Applying the profit margin formulas explicitly when discussing changes in GPM and NPM would strengthen methodological transparency.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Consumer behavior assertions (e.g., reluctance due to substitutes) are generic; support them with survey data or documented evidence.
The table caption appears below the data whereas academic convention usually places it above. Reposition the caption for consistency.
Include industry benchmark values directly beside Oreoâs GPM and NPM figures to contextualize performance trends.
The quarter label âQ3 202224â appears mistyped. Confirm and correct to âQ3 2022 4â or similar to avoid confusion.
Gross profit consistently exceeding sales revenue indicates a data error. Verify that sales revenue and profit figures are correctly placed.
The profit formulas in the table headings are correctly typeset in LaTeX, showing precise mathematical presentation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Footnotes reference multiple supporting documents in one line, making it unclear which figure relies on which source. Separate them clearly.
The conclusion succinctly answers the research question and refers back to all three analytical tools, ensuring consistency.
Introducing the war in Ukraine in the conclusion without prior analysis disrupts coherence. Focus conclusions on evidence already discussed.
The bibliography includes all sources but lacks consistent formatting style. Apply a standard citation format (APA/MLA) for uniformity.
Appendices headings are present but heading levels vary in size and style. Standardize heading formatting to enhance structure.
